title: node:test, Fixtures, Compile-Negative Cases, Faults, and Mutation Evidence · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers node:test, fixtures, compile-negative cases, faults, and mutation evidence into a changed risk-layered deterministic crawler test system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of risk-layered deterministic crawler test system with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, boundary, changed, Unicode, duplicate, inaccessible, and privacy-sensitive fixtures with provenance.
Test URL, robots, sitemap, state, scheduling, extraction, dedup, report, and recovery invariants with deterministic clocks and schedules.
Use expected TypeScript errors to prove invalid state, unsafe narrowing, mutable values, and missing exhaustive cases are rejected.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Fixture Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 5240 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove original fixtures, pure models, compile-negative tests, loopback faults, and mutation detection; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
